title: aira cespitosa subsp. paludosa
intro: Aira cespitosa subsp. paludosa, also known as marsh hair-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to Europe and is commonly found in wetlands, marshes, and floodplains. The medicinal properties of Aira cespitosa subsp. paludosa include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion for topical use.
